oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Picking up feet the old-fashioned way: How to diagnose and treat lameness when you haven’t got a trim chute
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Murphy, Anne
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: lame cows
restraint
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This presentation covers why there is a need to examine and treat lame cattle when a trim chute is not available, safety con­siderations when lifting feet without a chute, equipment used when a chute isn’t available, technique for lifting a hind foot, technique for lifting a front foot, options for sedation, and the usefulness of regional intravenous perfusion of lidocaine.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 117-119
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
